title: salix × elata
intro: Salix × elata, also known as the Korean weeping willow, is a hybrid plant species derived from the cross between Salix babylonica and Salix alba. The bark and leaves of the Salix × elata plant have been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asian cultures, due to their rich content of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The medicinal properties of Salix × elata include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and digestive issues. Salix × elata is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture to harness its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and promoting relaxation.
